channel.display-name: Weird or What? With William Shatner
The Yellow Couch with Jeremy Lynch
A New Life in the Sun

programme.title: Match Point
programme.desc: At a turning point in his life, a former tennis pro falls for an actress who happens to be dating his friend and soon-to-be brother-in-law.

programme.title: In Time
programme.desc: Justin Timberlake and Amanda Seyfried co-star in this sci-fi thriller set in a future world where aging stops at 25, and the only way for one man to stay alive is to steal more time.

programme.title: The Negotiator
programme.desc: The tables turn when a seasoned hostage negotiator takes hostages and risks all to prove his own innocence.

programme.title: Assassin's Creed
programme.desc: A man discovers he is a descendant of an ancient line of Assassins.

programme.title: The Double
programme.desc: An edge-of-your-seat espionage thriller, starring Richard Gere and Topher Grace as ex-CIA and FBI agents, trying to find the killer of a US senator, which bears the hallmark of an infamous Soviet assassin, thought to be dead.

programme.title: Hot Pursuit
programme.desc: In this riotous spin on the mismatched buddy movie, an uptight and by-the-book cop (Reese Witherspoon) tries to protect the gregarious widow (Sofía Vergara) of a drug boss as they race through Texas pursued by crooked cops and murderous gunmen!

programme.title: Dirty Work
programme.desc: A spiteful loser finds his true calling when he opens a highly successful business specializing in revenge.

programme.title: Wildlife
programme.desc: A teenage boy must deal with his mother's complicated response after his father temporarily abandons them to take a menial and dangerous job.

programme.title: The Internship
programme.desc: Old-school salesmen Billy (Vince Vaughn) and Nick (Owen Wilson) try to reboot their careers by joining Google’s fiercely competitive internship program — with hilarious results!

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Gore at War
programme.desc: Making a comeback is what Gore does best, but will his finisher be enough to vanquish Varis?

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Eclipsalypso / Apocalypse Squirt
programme.desc: McGee summons the Moon Dervish who will bring the Apocalypse unless a sasquatch dances for him.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Make way for the newest team of aerial super-stars: The Sky-Riders!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Tony returns to Earth through the Naroomi vortex relic in order to find the Core Dreamstone, while Edyn, Strag and the Magi Resistance Force battle Agram on the drained seabed of Orothe.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Down and out, losing all but one of the Guardian Hyrens they worked so hard to collect, the Final Dreamers rush to Cald to face off against Gorgram, and his army of Shadow Magi.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: When an astronaut/scientist discovers an alien embryo in a strange alien pod, he asks the celebrated Dr. Piranoid to come up to the space station to examine it -- not knowing that Piranoid plans to gene-slam Manta-ray DNA with alien DNA.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Come on down to Camp PoopyKaka, Master Mayhem’s new summer camp! It’s free, fun, and you can never leave! The golden overlord has enlisted his monsters as camp counselors, while Major Disappointment roasts planet Earth like a marshmallow.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Drift
programme.desc: Thrust flees into the depths of Cybertron, pursued by Hot Shot and Wheeljack - and, separately, Starscream, who saves the children from being killed. The children discover the origins of the minicons.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: When Trey’s estranged parents (medical workers on an aerial clinic vessel) crash-land in THE OUTBACK - a remote island prison colony where the worst criminals are living out a life sentence

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Storm Access
programme.desc: Playmaker’s life points are on the verge of elimination - along with any hopes of stopping Varis and the Tower of Hanoi!

programme.title: Kung Fu Dino Posse
programme.desc: Skor needs a brain to complete his latest creation, Frankendino. But the only brain that’s good enough is Edgar’s, so Skor hands Skrap a serrated brain spoon and sends him off to fetch it.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem is packing for a little vacation! He found a super cheap hotel, and while he’s away, the Earth will pay! He’s sending Macho Cheese to crumble Earth’s Champion and cover our planet in cheese… and cheesy jokes!

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: Every year dozens of Sky Knights go head to head in a treacherous bike race across Terra Saharr, competing to win a rare and powerful Velocity amulet.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Venturing into a Shadow Geyser in their pursuit of Korg and Zed – who transformed Orwin into a Shadow Magi and took him down to the Core to serve Agram - Tony, Edyn and Strag drop into a nightmarish maze inside the geyser.

programme.title: Storm Hawks
programme.desc: Having lost the Aurora Stone to the cunning Dark Ace, The Storm Hawks mount a daring mission, sneaking into the very heart of the evil empire of Cyclonia to take the powerful crystal back.

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem receives a visit from… himself! From the future?! With two MM’s in the mix, Earth is destined for doom... if they could just stop arguing. Meanwhile RoBro flexes his mechanical muscles in a Monster Mashdown that’ll go down in history.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: A new group of aerial daredevils, the Stunt-Hawks, have arrived for a series of shows in Soaring Heights, which coincides with a string of daring aerial heists throughout the city.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: In Naroom's Arena Ruins, Tony, Edyn and Strag are running battle drills in an attempt to master their still-raw Final Dreamer powers... when Furok begins to act strangely.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Tactician
programme.desc: A new transformer enters the solar system. On the moon, Thrust the master tactician arrives to assist Megatron in his battle against the Autobots, but all does not go according to plan.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: On the eve of a solar eclipse, General Raven travels to the great Pyramids of Egypt, to locate the artifact that will trigger an ancient Egyptian solar weapon capable of taking out any target.

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Dash
programme.desc: While the battle continues, Hot Shot goes to Cybertron to try to convince Galvatron of the impending danger. Thrust's true allegiance and agenda is revealed.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Standing Tall
programme.desc: The Tower of Hanoi is nearly complete – leaving Varis a win away from deleting LINK VRAINS once and for all!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Gale
programme.desc: A new minicon activates inside a highway tunnel, and the race is on to see who reach it first. Hot Shot is becoming more proficient with the Star Sabre. A new transformer arrives, claiming to be an Autobot, but Rad has his doubts.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Kolter’s Khronicles
programme.desc: The Hanoi have expanded their reach beyond the boundaries of LINK VRAINS, and Kolter’s chronicling the chaos!

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: Meta-Squadron has managed to capture Fly-Girl and Crusher, forcing Raven to offer a trade - the release of his agents for the return of a missing Metajets officer

programme.title: Massive Monster Mayhem
programme.desc: Master Mayhem has created a new chart-topping pop trio: The Doo Doo Dooms! These three musical monsters will melt your heart, then melt your planet! Meanwhile Eye Eye Eye will do anything for some peace and quiet… like conquer Earth!

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: The sharks accidentally get sent to the future where Piranoid has spread an air-born gene-slamming virus which has turned most of the world into subservient Seaviates. Only Bends' great great great grandson and a few other rebels didn't catch the virus.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Once Upon A Time
programme.desc: As Blue Angel's biggest fan, Spectre knows all her dueling tricks and techniques - which makes him the fiercest foe she's ever faced!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Korg's theft of a scroll from Naroom's library sends our heroes in pursuit of the Shadow Magi to the forbidding desert realm of the Sands of D’Resh. They ride aboard a Quanjuel – a horse-like creature that can travel through magical vortexes.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: In PART TWO, Paradigm offers to help the government scientists with the Street Sharks.

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: A battle between Gorgram, Korg & Zed and the Final Dreamers result in Gorgram being sent to the Dream Plane! But just when they all think they're rid of him forever, a clue from the Book of Elders hints that a Dream Plane Hyren exists…

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream
programme.desc: The gang finds themselves in the middle of a good ol' fashioned show down!
programme.sub-title: High Plains Garbage Eater / Dream a Little Scream

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Pranks for Nothing / Zombie Dearest
programme.desc: Eager to regain his lost prank mojo, McGee releases a mischievous pixy named Hanky Pranky.

programme.title: Street Sharks
programme.desc: The culmination of Dr. Piranoid's attempts at mass gene-slamming comes when he creates a fake vaccine which he claims will protect the citizens of Fission City against just such an attempt.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Fallen Angel
programme.desc: Playmaker’s battle takes a dark and dangerous turn when a malicious virus infects Blue Angel!

programme.title: Transformers: Armada: Crack
programme.desc: With the kids befriending and welcoming him, the Autobots deeply suspicious of him, and driven by his need to kill Megatron, the deeply conflicted Starscream trusts Thrust and returns to the moon base with the Star Sabre and Skyboom Shield.

programme.title: MetaJets
programme.desc: During a race at the floating Neptune Islands, the team encounters a squadron of old bomber jets that disappear in front of their eyes. The local urban legend says they are a ghostly squadron of jets that are trying to find their way home.

programme.title: Yu-Gi-Oh! VRAINS: Playback
programme.desc: Frog and Pigeon plan an exclusive expose on Playmaker after Ghost Gal supplies them with the goods on Link VRAINS’ newest hero!

programme.title: Magi-Nation
programme.desc: Tony's haunted dreams about his Grandpa lead the final dreamers to Orothe, where they find a surly, mean-tempered Spencer. But the kids are distracted by an unknown Shadow Magi, Mercenair, who is attacking the realm's powerful coral reef.

programme.title: World Poker Tour: WPT Legends of Poker
programme.desc: Six players compete for more than $750,000 at the WPT L.A. Poker Classic final table at Commerce Casino.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls are set the task of posing for the Debenhams Look Book under the watchful eye of designer Savannah Miller. They'll also have to think on their feet as they take part in a Kinky Boots Catwalk Challenge!

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The models take part in a charity photoshoot for animal rights charity PETA. This leaves many of the girls shocked and tearful as they realize they will be posing with dead animals. Will our girls be able to hold it together?

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: It's a hair-raising experience for the girls as they get their makeovers at this year's "Beauty Bootcamp." Overseen by celebrity stylist Paul Edmonds, our models get transformed from high street to high fashion.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The remaining girls take part in a runway challenge with a twist: teaching child models how to pose. Sparks fly at elimination when the girls are asked to vote for the weakest model. Who will stand out as a headliner and who will be yesterday's news?

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls have to pucker up for a photoshoot for Carmex as they lock lips with a hunky male model. After a tense week, which of our girls will kiss their modelling dream goodbye?

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The girls step it up in more ways than one in this week's fitness challenge for Fitbit as they take part in a fitness video. The girls then have to submerge themselves in freezing cold bath water in this week's photoshoot.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Being a top model involves constant travel and being camera-ready the moment you step off the plane. The girls are quickly put to work on location when they have to tackle the busy streets of Bangkok on several go-sees.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: Lights, camera, action! The girls film a commercial for Thai Airways where they'll be delivering their lines in Thai... or at least trying to. Later, they enter the ring for a Muay Thai-themed photoshoot.

programme.title: Britain's Next Top Model
programme.desc: The final three girls head back to the UK for a runway show at London Fashion Week Festival. With the pressure at its peak, who will crumble and who will rise above it all to be crowned Britain's Next Top Model?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 190
programme.desc: Can fireworks really launch a person over a lake? Can the weight of a bird be enough to tip a teetering car off of a cliff? Can a water heater turned on its side be used as a cannon?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 208
programme.desc: Can man beat machine in a 30-foot, standing-start sprint? Can a driver lose 10lbs during a race? Can the suction created by a speeding car rip loose a manhole cover during a street race?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 35
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a jet pack be created from plans on the internet? Do pyramids have special powers?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 135
programme.desc: Will opening all of the windows of a house during a hurricane lessen the structure's damage? Will a human head that has been dipped in liquid nitrogen for 5 seconds shatter when slammed into a table?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 150
programme.desc: Can someone really be knocked out of their socks? Is the force exerted between two cars crashing into each other at 50mph the same as a single car hitting a wall at 50mph?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 38
programme.desc: Myths tested: Is it possible to slingshot a person over the border and onto a mattress?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 164
programme.desc: Can honey bees lift a laptop? Can hitting a bug at high speeds on a motorcycle be fatal? Can hanging a bag of water repel flies?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 16
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person take to the skies using only a lawn chair and weather balloons? Can someone test positive for heroin by eating a large amount of poppy seeds?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 195
programme.desc: Can you survive a 35-foot fall while covered in bubble wrap? Can a rocket-powered ejector seat flip an upside-down car back onto its wheels, as seen in "Die Another Day"?

programme.title: MythBusters: Buster Special
programme.desc: This special episode pays homage to Buster the Crash Test Dummy. Without him, either dozens of myths would have gone untested or Jamie and Adam would constantly be at the local hospital.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 88
programme.desc: Myths tested: Do birds flying in a trailer cause the trailer to become lighter? Will a boat crashing into a channel marker cleave itself in half?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 203
programme.desc: Can a motorcycle traveling at highway speeds drive across the surface of a lake? Can you survive a jump from a high-rise building with a parachute made from a hotel room's contents?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 3
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a barrel of bricks from the top of a building hit a man multiple times? Can a person be electrocuted by urinating on the third rail?

programme.title: Mythbusters Outtakes Special
programme.desc: It’s a shame that not all of the testing footage can be included in the final show that goes to air. In this special episode, the outtakes are in with never-before-seen footage.

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 27
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a person be launched by rockets strapped to a chair, like in the Ming Dynasty tale? Can jumping into a ceiling fan cause a person to lose his head?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 18
programme.desc: Myths tested: Can a scuba diver be sucked up in a firefighting helicopter? Are various car myths from movies true?

programme.title: MythBusters: Episode 168
programme.desc: Can realistic facial masks be used to bypass security measures as shown in the "Mission: Impossible" television series? Is it possible to start a merry-go-round spinning by shooting bullets at it?

programme.title: Black Knight
programme.desc: When a wisecracking black hustler from South Central L.A. finds himself transported back to medieval England, he leads the overthrow of an evil king and wins the heart of a proto feminist woman.

programme.title: Meet Dave
programme.desc: A crew of miniature aliens operate a spaceship that has a human form

programme.title: Spaceballs
programme.desc: Space hero Lone Starr (Bill Pullman) and his half-canine buddy Barf (John Candy) are on a mission in their flying Winnebago to save Princess Vespa (Daphne Zuniga) from the clutches of Dark Helmet (Rick Moranis) in Mel Brooks' inspired sci-fi spoof!

programme.title: Trees Lounge
programme.desc: A man drifting through time desperately searches for some meaning to his life.

programme.title: Bring Me The Head of Mavis Davis
programme.desc: Music producer Marty discovers that the woman he turned into a household name, Mavis Davis is losing her fame. The only way to secure her fame is for her to die. The problem is, she refuses to die.

programme.title: Ghost World
programme.desc: The story of neo-cool Enid and Rebecca who, faced with graduation from high school take a hard look at the world they wryly observe. When Rebecca focuses her attention on mutual romantic fixation Josh, the girls friendship is forever changed.

programme.title: Big Mommas: Like Father, Like Son
programme.desc: Malcolm Turner and his stepson Trent go undercover at an all-girls performing arts school

programme.title: Memoirs Of An Invisible Man
programme.desc: When a government experiment goes awry it renders an innocent bystander invisible.

programme.title: Superhero Movie
programme.desc: An orphaned teenager is bitten by a radioactive dragonfly, granting him superhuman powers in this parody of films like Spider-Man, X-Men, and Fantastic Four.

programme.title: Moonstruck
programme.desc: In her Academy Award winning role, Cher, while waiting for her timid fiance to decide between her and his mother, falls in love with her fiance's estranged brother, a bakery worker with a love of opera and life.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: On an archeological dig, Matt and scientists come upon a mysterious gorge where their cars have been crushed.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Ghost and Mr. Panther': Pinky unknowingly takes a job as a house sitter…at a haunted house! 'Cleopanthra': Pinky wants to make it as an architect in ancient Egypt but must contend with the Royal Architect who is out to destroy his work.

programme.title: Count Duckula
programme.desc: Duckula tries to impress the nanny fan club by winning all the winter sports.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Penfold types up his memoirs. Colonel K tells them that things around the city are suddenly vanishing with a loud "kazonk". Then Danger Mouse and Penfold are kazonked into a void-like dimension by Dr. Crumhorn.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: '14 Karat Pink': The Panther tries to claim the Lollapalooza Gold Vein but Manly Man has other ideas. 'Robo-Pink': While working as a Security Guard at a super secret factory, the Panther thwarts the Dogfather's plans to steal a secret 'doodad.'

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Matt discovers an empty submarine controlled by Brian the Brain, a computer he had given grant money for, and now in the clutches of Mayhem and the VENOM gang.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Bill and Ted find themselves with a problem: how to get part-time jobs when they have no experience. They manage to get references from Marie Antoinette and Lucretia Borgia but decide that working on music is preferable to working at a real job.
programme.sub-title: A Job, A Job, My Kingdom For A Job

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Cowboy Pink': Panther helps Heidi Von Schmarty get her herd of cows to San Francisco, despite cattle rustling attempts by Dogfather. 'Stealth Panther': Panther goes on a secret mission to recover a stolen Stealth aircraft from a group of bad guys.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback has a device that transmits waves to brainwash people. He brainwashes Colonel K to send Danger Mouse and Penfold on a mission to the South Pole, where they have trouble with a mechanical wild goose that lays explosive eggs.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: During an investigation where Danger Mouse and Penfold try to find out why elephants are being turned into sugar cubes, a fall causes Penfold to act weird and do a Tarzan yell that summons elephants to stampede the area.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: The Australian Outback is the setting for a true Battle of the Giants. The Mask Team is pitted against a Venom Team.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Voodoo Man': When Voodoo Man's island is wiped out by a storm, he goes in search of the weatherman he believes is responsible. '7 Manly Men': When the toy in a box of cereal doesn't live up to its promise, the Manly Men confront the company owner.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Matt, Scott and T-Bob's trip on the Orient Express is interrupted when three doors are stolen from the compartments that used to belong to King Ludwig.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Mayhem steals an ancient skull which enables anyone looking through its eyes to see through solid object.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Rest is History
programme.desc: Duckula travels back in time to stop the first Count Duckula from becoming a vampire.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: Mr. Logan accuses Ted of not being a perfect teenager like he was and threatens to ship him off to military school. To learn self-discipline, Bill and Ted visit Mr. Logan during his high school days.
programme.sub-title: Apple Doesn't Fall Far From The Phone Booth

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback steals a growth serum from Professor von Squawkencluck and with it creates giant chickens to take over the world once and for all. And for once, Penfold saves the day!

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Life in the Pink Lane
programme.desc: First time bowler Pink Panther throws pro-bowler Big Nose off his game.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: London appears to be flooded in water, and its citizens have to crowd up in the top parts of its buildings.

programme.title: Count Duckula: A Christmas Quacker
programme.desc: Duckula is visited by both Father Christmas and Von Goosewing at the Castle on Christmas Day.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: A Fairly Pink Pumpkin
programme.desc: Farmer Big Nose dreams of winning the pumpkin contest at the fair, but can he beat Pink Panther's pumpkin?

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Legend of El Pinko': When the Headless Jalapeno terrorizes the people of an old California village, El Pinko must come to the rescue. 'Pink Big': While working as a lab assistant to professor Von Schmarty, Pinky develops a super growth formula.
programme.sub-title: Legend Of El Pinko, The/Pink Big

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Pinky's Dilemma': Pinky's pen pal Queen Kong makes a surprise visit to the big city and his life is turned upside down when a cyclone hits. 'Oh Varkula': The Panther spends the night at a castle Varkula defending the ant from Count Varkula.

programme.title: Bill & Ted's Excellent Adventures
programme.desc: An overdue library book comes between the boys and a ski trip. Bill and Ted travel back in history to find the Brothers Grimm and a copy of the book. Bill and Ted take them on an adventure through time.
programme.sub-title: A Grimm Story Of An Overdue Book

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Deep in the Yucatan jungle at an ancient Mayan City a boy is seen lifting a very heavy stone with ease.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM steals an anti-gravity ray, terrorizes aircraft, and threatens to wipe out cities unless a ransom is paid.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Dr Crumhorn returns and uses his transformation pills to turn himself into a little girl in an attempt to persuade Danger Mouse and Penfold to help her out and eventually lead them to break into Fort Knox.

programme.title: Count Duckula: No Yaks Please, We're Tibetan
programme.desc: The trio travel to Tibet

programme.title: The Wombles
programme.desc:
Orinoco goes for a spin on a windy day.
(Orinoco And The Big Black Umbrella / The Rocking Chair / A Sticky End / Great Uncle Bulgaria’s Keep-Fit Lesson)
programme.sub-title: Orinoco And The Big Black Umbrella / The Rocking Chair / A Sticky End / Great Uncle Bulgaria’s...

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Pinky Up The River': The Panther opens a river rafting operation only to encounter competition from Manly Man. 'Long John Pinky': The Panther gets shanghaied into being the cook aboard a pirate ship and must outsmart the dreaded Captain R. Gyle.
programme.sub-title: Pinky Up The River/Long John Pinky

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'The Inspector's Club': the Inspector accepts a challenge to solve the mystery of the mythical lost city of Atlantis. 'A Royal Pain': Bellhop Pinky stands in for a visiting 10-year-old king at a King's Convention and nearly starts World War III.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'All For Pink And Pink For All': Pinky, the Ant and Aardvark must save the Queen from the clutches of the evil Richelieu. 'Service With A Pink': An unscrupulous hotel boss forces Bellboy Panther to enter the wrestling ring with a vicious wrestler.
programme.sub-title: All For Pink And Pink For All/Service With A Pink Smile

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: The Pink The Bad And The Ugly
programme.desc: Sheriff Pink and Hoarse must rescue the town's money from Bandit Big Nose and his gang.
programme.sub-title: The Pink The Bad And The Ugly

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold had to the China seas to look for Professor Squakencluck who, despite having invented an object that can never be lost, ends up dropping the item in a remote island and getting lost himself.

programme.title: Count Duckula: The Ghost of McCastle McDuckula
programme.desc: Duckula, Igor and Nanny, on holiday in Scotland hope to stay at the Glen Sparrows Hotel, but Igor has other ideas.

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: Masquerading as an evil giant, VENOM sets up a giant magnetic force on a tropical island which pulls a freighter loaded with platinum right onto shore.

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Colonel K teams Danger Mouse up with a robot Penfold, and Penfold with a robot Danger Mouse. The robots prove to be a lot of trouble for the real ones, taking instructions too literally or getting stuck running in circles.
programme.sub-title: Four Heads Are Better Than Two

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Greenback kidnaps Penfold to get Danger Mouse to bring him four ingredients needed for an evil spell to take over the world: The hair of a yeti, straw from a witch's broom, a piece of the London fog monster, and the tail feathers of Count Duckula.
programme.sub-title: The Four Tasks of Danger Mouse

programme.title: M.A.S.K.
programme.desc: VENOM has a gravity force that smashes things flat.

programme.title: Pink Panther & Pals: Pink Trek
programme.desc: Pink Panther and Hoarse race Big Nose and Dog in a competition to see who will be promoted from janitor to postman.

programme.title: THE NEW PINK PANTHER SHOW
programme.desc: 'Dino Sour Head': An inventive prehistoric Pink Panther must help rid his village of a pesky Tyrannosaur who is causing trouble. 'The Luck o' The Pinkish': While hunting for a four-leaf clover, Pink runs afoul of a mischievous Leprechaun.
programme.sub-title: Dino Sour Head/The Luck O' The Pinkish

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 26
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a bungalow in Devon with hidden depths, a house in Dover and a property in Preston with a problem in the cellar.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 25
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a commercial property with a flat above it in Derby, a one bed masionette in Sheerness, Kent and a mid terrace in Blackburn.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 6
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a bungalow in Wiltshire, a three bedroomed house in Hertfordshire and a semi detached house in Stoke on Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 14
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts go to Stoke-on-Trent to see a 2 bed room mid-terrace. In Streatham a house with lots of space and lots of potential; and finally in Kent a 2 bedroom developers dream.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy view properties Exeter, Isle of Wight and Wales.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 10
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a burnt out three bedroomed house in Salford, a four bed semi detached house in London and in a tiny property in Devon.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 20
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a house in Derby, a chapel in Chelmsford and a flat in London.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a flat in Surrey, a house in Derbyshire and a house in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 21
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts visit a property in Stockport near Manchester to see if can make a profit, a neglected house in Derbyshire which benefits from spectacular views, and a bungalow in Kent which has suffered severe water damage.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in South London, Cornwall and Cheshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 9
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit a three bedroomed house in London, a two bedroomed house in Cornwall and a three bedroom flat in Wiltshire.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 23
programme.desc: Martin & Lucy visit an old store in Dorset, a three bedroom detached house in Hampshire and a commercial property in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit properties in Manchester and Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 26
programme.desc: Lucy and Martin visit gem in the Cornish countryside is far from the madding crowd, in London there's more to this one bedroomed flat that meets the eye and there are tempting opportunities in South Wales.

programme.title: Homes Under The Hammer: Episode 2
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a house in Stoke that may require some nimble footwork, a property in Berkshire proving how much size does matter and a rather tumble down building in Devon.

programme.title: 12 Angry Men
programme.desc: Eleven jurors are convinced that the defendant is guilty of murder. The twelfth has no doubt of his innocence. How can this one man steer the others toward the same conclusion? Henry Fonda and Lee J. Cobb lead the all-star cast in this classic!

programme.title: Laurel & Hardy: Berth Marks
programme.desc: Stanley and Oliver stumble into the wrong sleeping compartment on the train to Pottsville.

programme.title: Laurel & Hardy: Hog Wild
programme.desc: Hog Wild is a 1930 American pre-Code Laurel and Hardy film, directed by James Parrott.

programme.title: Brazil
programme.desc: In the retro-future world, a government bureaucrat tries to correct an administrative error and quickly finds himself an enemy of the state.

programme.title: The Vampire's Night Orgy
programme.desc: Stranded travelers seek refuge in a remote village, only to discover it's inhabited by vampires with sinister intentions.

programme.title: Satan's Cheerleaders
programme.desc: The janitor at a local high school is actually the scout for a coven of Satanists on the lookout for a virgin to sacrifice. One day he kidnaps the cheerleading squad to use for their rituals. However, one of the cheerleaders is actually a witch.

programme.title: Diary of a Nudist
programme.desc: A reporter infiltrates a nudist camp for a story but finds herself embracing the lifestyle, challenging her own beliefs and societal expectations.

programme.title: The Bat
programme.desc: A crazed killer known as "The Bat" is on the loose in a mansion full of people.

programme.title: The Plague Dogs
programme.desc: A stunning animated adaptation of Richard Adams' novel, The Plague Dogs follows a pair of dogs, Snitter and Rowf, who escape from a research laboratory and try to survive in the wild with the help of a cunning fox.

programme.title: Find the Lady
programme.desc: Two gun toting hoodlums, Trigger (Mickey Rooney) & Leo (Dick Emery) are hired by the unscrupulous J.K. (Peter Cook) to kidnap his niece Victoria. What follows is lunacy on a scale rarely seen!

programme.title: The Raven
programme.desc: The sorcerer Craven learns from fellow magician Dr. Bedlo that his supposedly dead wife is alive and living at the castle of master sorcerer Dr. Scarabus.

programme.title: That Lucky Touch
programme.desc: A pacifist newswoman covering a NATO meeting becomes entangled with her neighbor, an international arms dealer.

programme.title: Euronews
programme.desc: Euronews offers a unique perspective on world events. It empowers people to make up their own mind by delivering factual analysis and a diversity of viewpoints. Because all views matter, Euronews is "All Views".

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women never get their hands dirty: no blood touched them because they manipulate others to kill for them...but they didn't get away.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: In some cases, the transformation from everyday woman to brutal killer is activated by a man. A boyfriend or husband can become the focus of a deadly obsession.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women burn madly with jealousy, an anger so deep they’ll kill to satisfy it. What makes them turn from “green with envy” to “lethal with jealousy?" Find out as we explore their hearts of darkness.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: A lovely lady with an innocent smile, kind eyes, a charming personality? No one would suspect a woman like that would murder. These women don’t look or behave like killers.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Under His Control
programme.desc: These are love stories with bleak, bloody endings. Blinded by desire, obsessed and malleable, some women fall into lethal relationships with dangerous, controlling men.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: No one doubts a woman’s capacity to love, but sometimes lust can turn lethal. In the ultimate fatal attraction, "Deadly Women" looks at cases of murder driven by obsessive love.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Women are more often prey than predators, but the exceptions are deadly women indeed. In this episode, we’ll discover what drove these deadly women to prey on the unsuspecting.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Greed is one of the oldest motives for murder, and these deadly women are no exception: they’ll do anything to satisfy their need for cash. This is the dangerous world of the fortune hunters.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Greed
programme.desc: Known as “black widows,” these women kill their spouses, lovers and children for profit. Murder for them becomes a business. Their trade is murder. Their profit? Blood money.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Crimes of revenge are usually single acts of passion. A brew of hatred and restitution boils over in a manic moment of fury. Once caught, revenge killers usually display remorse and atonement.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: The path towards revenge can sometimes lead to murder. It can be quick, fiery and violent. It can also be a dish served cold, the product of a calm, calculated plot.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Sometimes, when a man and a woman unite, a terrible urge for destruction can awaken in them both. Happy couples become deadly teams, intent on one goal: murder.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While marriage is meant to be for life, it often ends in divorce. These wives took their marriages vow seriously: "till death do us part."

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: Blood ties are strong ties, sometimes strong enough to pull a person into a murderous conspiracy. When a family member wields his or her power, the result can be death.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: While their sisters chose a life of sensible, suburban motherhood, these deadly women chose a life of crime. They lived with the thrills, the danger and the deadly consequences of being outlaws.

programme.title: Deadly Women: Married to Murder
programme.desc: To the outside world, their marriages seemed happy. But behind closed doors, there was no domestic bliss. These women wanted out, but getting a divorce wasn’t their preferred option.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: We want to believe that all women would give their lives to protect their children. But there are horrifying cases when a mother will turn against her natural instinct and kill her own flesh and blood.

programme.title: Deadly Women
programme.desc: These women would stop at nothing to get their hands on the cash they needed. From husbands to strangers to other people’s babies, they spilt blood for money.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Blood Brothers
programme.desc: When the small community of Noel, Missouri discovered their bank was robbed, they're shocked. When the bank president turned up missing, they're outraged. And when clues began to surface the facts were more terrible than anyone could have guessed.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Murdering Cowboy
programme.desc: The sun set on the Wild West long ago, but some people refused to notice. Claude Dallas was one of them. A wily trapper, Dallas typified the rugged individualism that forged this country. He went too far when he killed two game wardens in 1981.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Predator
programme.desc: In the early 1990's, Washington, D.C., became the murder capital of the nation. Few were left untouched by gang violence, which was ignited by the drug trade. By 1994, that violence would strike at the very heart of law enforcement.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Dead Run
programme.desc: In Pennsylvania, an inmate escaped with the help of his female accomplice. Following a trail of fraudulent credit card receipts and deaths, authorities track the pair, but always find themselves one step behind.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Deadly Stranger
programme.desc: The body of a young woman found burned in a truck launched a manhunt to find her killer. When more bodies began to turn up across the country, the FBI believed the cases were linked. Investigators had a suspect. They just couldn't find him.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Small Town Terror
programme.desc: In a small Ohio community, an elderly woman and a teenaged boy disappeared. Because they vanished within hours of each other, police believed the cases were linked. But they didn't know how.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The True Story of Mississippi Burning
programme.desc: Philadelphia, Mississippi, 1964. A sleepy southern town steeped in tradition finds itself targeted for invasion.
programme.sub-title: The True Story of Mississippi Burning

programme.title: The FBI Files: Death in Alaska
programme.desc: The day began like any other for Cheryl and Paul Chapman, until the silence of a peaceful Sunday morning was shattered with a phone call.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Temple of Fear
programme.desc: In Miami, the discovery of a mutilated body sent investigators on the trail of a deadly cult. But agents found a wall of silence around the cult's members. Potential witnesses, terrified of reprisals, refused to talk.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Radical Agenda
programme.desc: In the 1970's a string of bank robberies and bombings spread fear and destruction along the east coast. The FBI faced a new breed of criminal, the domestic terrorist.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Killer Abroad
programme.desc: Beginning in 1990, Europe found itself in the clutches of a serial killer. He became an international outlaw, hopping from country to country to evade capture and to kill again.

programme.title: The FBI Files: The Search For Lisa Rene
programme.desc: When a young woman was grabbed from her Texas home, the police raced to find her abductors. On the face of it, the kidnapping seemed like the random act of desperate men.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Alabama, two desperate criminals fled the custody of a local deputy. Eluding authorities, they made their way across the country in a wave of car theft, abduction, and murder.

programme.title: The FBI Files: .22 Caliber Killer
programme.desc: In Buffalo, New York, a sniper targeted African-American men. He held a city hostage, and threatened to ignite a racial powder keg. And it looked like others might be following his bloody example.

programme.title: The FBI Files: A Model Killer
programme.desc: It's a model's job to rivet the public's attention. But in 1984, some aspiring models began attracting publicity in a terrible new way.

programme.title: The FBI Files: Hidden Agenda
programme.desc: Across the Midwest, a band of armed robbers struck banks at the rate of nearly one per month. They attacked with lightning precision and lethal force. They were in and out in five minutes.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan must reverse course when efforts to make Klink look competent before the Nazi Inspector General get the Kommandant promoted with a transfer to Berlin.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan's efforts to help a German baroness to escape are thwarted by Klink, who has arranged for the transfer of a British prisoner who is senior to Colonel Hogan.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: When a British commando unit is captured before they can blow up a crucial German ammo dump, Hogan and his men attempt to finish the job.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: In order to steal enough explosives to blow up a difficult bridge, Hogan's men paint anti-Nazi graffiti on the camp's ammo building.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan wants to get a peek at the documents that a visiting general has.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan convinces Kommandant Klink that the heavy water being stored at Stalag 13 is a youth potion.
programme.sub-title: Go Light On The Heavy Water

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: After Klink finds Sergeant Schultz drunk he gets a tough new replacement.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Corporal LeBeau pretends to be a visiting French scientist while Hogan smuggles out the real one.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan sneaks out of camp to a local German Hofbrau House to get the details of a nearby German army unit.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The Germans want to safely build a synthetic fuel plant in Stalag 13, but Hogan convinces them that there is oil beneath the camp.
programme.sub-title: Oil For The Lamps Of Hogan

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: During an operation to send out twenty Allied soldiers, two of them attempt an early escape and are nearly captured.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan talks Klink into building an officers' club, whereby a boat can be built to sail away with an escaped British POW and the German gunsight he snagged while escaping.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan and his men must destroy a German anti-aircraft artillery unit so that the Allies can send in a bombing raid on Hitler's birthday.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: The Germans have stolen a gold shipment from the French and Hogan must convince Klink to have it moved to the safety of Stalag 13.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: While Hogan detains a German general during an Allied offensive, a Gestapo officer looks into Stalag 13's perfect escape record.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan's latest underground contacts are secretly German spies.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Sergeant Schultz impersonates Klink in a scheme to get some of his men free from the Gestapo.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: To get an Italian officer to defect, the Heroes use pizza to tempt him.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: Hogan has a mission to move a mobile anti-aircraft artillery battery, but Klink's new tough second-in-command is impeding the Heroes.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: A new prisoner in a corporal's uniform turns out to be a general with a mission for Hogan.
programme.sub-title: How To Cook A German Goose With Radar

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: In a plot to get to an airplane with a silent engine, Hogan gets Klink to believe that he is psychic.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: An African prince comes to Stalag 13 who is negotiating with the Germans for the rights in his country to build a submarine base and Hogan replaces him with Kinchloe.
programme.sub-title: The Prince From The Phone Company

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes
programme.desc: As Hogan tries to help a German scientist defect, bumbling Colonel Crittendon interferes again.

programme.title: Hogan's Heroes

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: MYSTERIES OF HIDDEN ROOMS
programme.desc: Throughout history, humanity has been captivated by hidden places—rooms, passages, and chambers. Whether for escape or sinister motives, these ten notable hidden spaces span the ages, revealing intrigue and mystery.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: Ron Wyatt, a Nashville nurse turned amateur archaeologist at 47, aimed to validate Bible stories. In 1977, he claimed finding Noah’s Ark on Mount Ararat and later dived for Egyptian chariot remains, supporting Moses’ Red Sea crossing.
programme.sub-title: Raider Ron and the Lost Ark

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: California Gold Rush prospector goes in search of a legendary lost treasure ship in the Mojave Desert.
programme.sub-title: The Lost Ship of the Mojave Desert

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: According to legend, King Arthur was Britain’s greatest king. He led his Knights of the Round Table from Camelot, a spectacular castle, and fought Anglo-Saxon invaders, uniting the country. Leslie Alcock led a team to find Camelot.

programme.title: Myth Hunters
programme.desc: A local newspaper editor and businessman in South Africa unwittingly finds himself at the epicentre of a legend of buried gold that began during the Boer War.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: JFK
programme.desc: Richard meets with some prominent JFK assassination researchers to discuss the November 1963 murder of President John F. Kennedy.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Remote Viewing
programme.desc: We meet several individuals who claim they have the ability to transcend time and space and identify people, objects, and locations hidden from their view.

programme.desc: The Allies defeated the Axis Powers on the battlefield, but did they actually win the war? Richard interviews several alternative historians who say that while the German Army may have surrendered in 1945, the Nazi's never officially did.
programme.sub-title: The Rise of the Fourth Reich (Nazi's in America)

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Murder of Marilyn Monroe
programme.desc: Did this pop icon and blond bombshell succumb to a drug overdose in 1962 as we've been led to believe or was she killed for 'knowing too much?"

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett

programme.sub-title: Journey to the Center of the Earth

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Shroud of Turin
programme.desc: Is this famous piece of linen cloth the actual burial cloth of Jesus Christ or a clever Medieval hoax?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Energy Healing
programme.desc: Several energy healers claim they are able to produce spontaneous miraculous healing through a laying on of hands. Claims of similar such healing by a man in a remote village in Brazil, known as John of God are also examined.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: What on Earth are they spraying? Richard travels into the field to meet several researchers who present compelling evidence that chemicals or biological agents are being deliberately sprayed into the atmosphere by some shadowy government agency.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets members of the 9/11 Truth movement who challenge the official version of events concerning the attack on the U.S. Pentagon. Researchers present evidence to suggest the attack was perpetrated by some rogue element inside the U.S. government.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Were the Mercury, Genesis, Apollo and Space Shuttle programs mere window dressing designed for public consumption?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard speaks with the founders of Architects and Engineers for 911 Truth and Firefighters for 911 Truth who present their case that the WTC towers were brought down by controlled demolition on 9/11.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A serious investigation of the long-held theory that Elvis Presley faked his death in August 1977. Jokes of Elvis sightings in Kalamazoo aside, there is a considerable volume of evidence to suggest Elvis may not have left the building

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: A lawyer and from the Pacific Northwest claims to have been part of a secret government time travel experiment, known as Project Pegasus.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: GMO - Genetically Modified Food
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims crop plants created for human or animal consumption using the latest molecular biology techniques are unsafe.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard investigates claims that the supposed mastermind behind the 9/11 terrorist attacks and leader of the Al Qaeda terrorist cell remained a CIA asset until his death.

programme.title: Top 10: Secrets and Mysteries: SECRET TECHNOLOGIES

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2007, Meredith Kercher is murdered in Italy. Amanda Knox and Raffaele Sollecito are initially convicted but later exonerated. Rudy Guede is convicted. The Kercher family still seeks justice.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.
programme.sub-title: Death of a Mail Order Bride

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2004, Marc Dutroux was sentenced to life for kidnapping, raping, and murdering young girls in Belgium. His 1996 arrest and delayed trial exposed horrific crimes and official failings, sparking public outrage.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 28, 2005, Joan Porco attends the trial of her son Christopher, accused of murdering his father and attacking her. Despite her initial identification and later refusal to testify, Christopher is found guilty based on circumstantial evidence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Murder by the Mob depicts the battle for justice by Peppino Impastato's family after his 1978 death was misclassified. His mother and brother reopened the case, leading to mafia boss Gaetano Badalamenti's 2002 conviction for the murder.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, Ashley Pond and Miranda Gaddis disappear in Portland. Neighbor Ward Weaver, a suspect with a violent history, is later found guilty of their murders and sentenced to life in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: A picture-perfect Canadian couple Karla Homolka and Paul Bernardo join to perform torture and mutilation on young victims - crimes that are virtually unspeakable. Two teenage girls were raped and murdered, and the couple even filmed themselves doing it.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On June 27, 2005, Dennis Rader, known as the BTK killer, pleads guilty to ten murders he committed in Wichita, Kansas, from 1974 to 1994. After a journalist's article reignites the case, Rader is arrested and sentenced to ten life terms without parole.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In September 1987, Simon Dale is murdered. His ex-wife, Susan Wilberforce, is acquitted of the crime but later convicted of fraud and sentenced to seven years. The murder remains unsolved.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In January 2002, fashion journalist Christa Worthington is found murdered in her Truro home. Christopher McCowen is convicted in 2006 of her murder, robbery, and rape, despite his claims of innocence and other DNA found at the scene.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1999, NFL player Rae Carruth is convicted for orchestrating the shooting of his pregnant girlfriend. He is sentenced to 18-24 years. Their son, Chancellor, has cerebral palsy.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In February 1977, Australian heiress Janie Shepherd vanishes in London. David Lashley, a convicted rapist, later confesses to her murder in prison. In 1990, he is convicted based on this confession and sentenced to life.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1998, Peter Bergna's car crashes off a mountain, killing his wife Rinette. After suspicions arise and a first trial ends in a hung jury, Bergna is convicted of murder in 2002 following new testimony about his abusive behavior.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1975, 15-year-old Martha Moxley is murdered in Belle Haven, Connecticut. The case goes cold until 2002, when Michael Skakel, a family member, is convicted based on new evidence. Skakel maintains his innocence.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On December 7, 1993, Colin Ferguson killed six and injured nineteen in the Long Island Railroad Massacre. Defending himself at trial, he bizarrely questioned his own victims and was sentenced to over 300 years in prison.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1995 Paris, Guy Georges, a serial killer, was linked to multiple murders through DNA and later confessed in court. He was sentenced to life in prison, leading to the establishment of a DNA database for offenders in France.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1990, Danny Rolling killed five students in Gainesville, Florida. He confessed to the crimes, pleaded guilty to avoid the death penalty, but was ultimately sentenced to death and executed 16 years later.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In a gruesome 1989 case, Tracey Wigginton and her accomplices lure Edward Baldock with promises of a good time, only to brutally stab him to death, sparking a sensational trial that raised questions about the roles of her accomplices.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In July 2000, Sarah Payne is abducted and murdered. Roy Whiting, a convicted sex offender, is found guilty and sentenced to life in 2001. Sarah’s case leads to the implementation of “Sarah’s Law” in the UK in 2011.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: Nathaniel Bar-Jonah, a paedophile, is convicted in 2002 for multiple crimes but not for the 1996 disappearance of Zachary Ramsay. Zachary’s body is never found, and Bar-Jonah dies in prison in 2008.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2001, British traveler Peter Falconio is presumed murdered in the Australian outback. Joanne Lees survives the attack, and Bradley John Murdoch is convicted of the murder in 2005. Peter's body is never found.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2007, Meredith Kercher is murdered in Italy. Amanda Knox and Raffaele Sollecito are initially convicted but later exonerated. Rudy Guede is convicted. The Kercher family still seeks justice.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 2006, Nina Sharanova vanished in Oakland. Her ex-husband, Hans Reiser, was convicted of her murder after their son’s testimony and leading police to her body. Reiser is now appealing the conviction.
programme.sub-title: Death of a Mail Order Bride

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: This is the tragic tale of Susan Smith, who shocked the nation in 1994 by fabricating a carjacking to cover up the drowning murders of her two young sons.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: University students Holly Dunn and Chris Maier are ambushed along railway tracks in August 1997. By 1999, a string of similar attacks leads police to Angel Maturino Reséndiz, who travels by freight train between murders across three states.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1983, Graeme Cunningham vanishes in Salt Lake City. Detective Don Bell discovers Arthur Bishop, who confesses to killing Graeme and four other boys. Bishop is found guilty in 1984 and executed in 1988.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: In 1991, Ray and Faye Copeland, the oldest death row couple in the USA, are convicted of murdering five drifters. Police find the bodies after a tip-off. Faye's death sentence is commuted to life in 1999.

programme.title: Great Crimes and Trials
programme.desc: On October 11, 1998, Dr. Harold Shipman is tried for murdering fifteen elderly patients. Exhumation and evidence of lethal morphine lead to his conviction. He is later found to have killed 215 people.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Something foreboding"
programme.desc: Sara, Phil and the Are You Haunted team head to South Yorkshire, where a venue with military connections awaits investigation. Based on personal experiences, will the team think this venue is haunted, or not; and will you agree with their decision?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Hello? I am here!"

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Freaky as hell!"
programme.desc: In this episode, the Are You Haunted team investigates a venue with a rather sinister history. Could this be tied into the reports of possible paranormal activity reported here? Plus, Dale and Ben investigate a mysterious 'room'; what was it used for?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Total creep out!"
programme.desc: The Are You Haunted team is back this time with a rather unusual haunted venue, providing a very uneasy atmosphere. With reports of phantom footsteps, unexplained voices, and a full apparition. But what will the team uncover for themselves?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "A very strange place"
programme.desc: The Are You Haunted team investigates a venue with some amazing history and some bizarre incidents. Everything from unexplained footsteps to apparitions has been witnessed here; and for two members of the crew, it will be a venue they won't forget.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Step out of the darkness"
programme.desc: The team investigate a venue with a fascinating history and colourful past. With unexplained noises being reported regularly, as well as ghostly forms, the team makes a disturbing discovery in their pitch-black surroundings: they are not alone.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Did someone just talk?"
programme.desc: The team head to Leeds to investigate a fascinating, historical building. With reports of unexplained footsteps, people being touched, and an apparition of a former staff member, the AYH team have a lot to investigate.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Hide and seek"
programme.desc: An old venue with a different past awaits the team in this episode, where among other reported incidents, the ghost of a mischievous little boy has been seen and heard, playing. Plus, voices and unexplained footsteps have been encountered.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: "Fight or flight"
programme.desc: In the finale of season two, the team spend the night at a former RAF base. Bangs on request, footsteps, and more are encountered; and the venue has a strange effect on some of the team, with one AYH member getting the fright of their lives.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Unfinished business
programme.desc: The Are You Haunted..? team are back with the first episode kicking of a brand new season. Three more venues are put before the team, only one will be investigated: but which one?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: A different time
programme.desc: In this episode, the AYH team visit a building that delivers much more than initially meets the eye. Poltergeist activity, unexplained noises and vocal sounds are all experienced...but at which venue?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Something behind me!
programme.desc: The team are back once again. This episode sees the intrepid team joined by two guest investigators - Kevin Ling and Michael Roberts - as they investigate a reputedly haunted venue bigger than most!

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Who's down there?
programme.desc: The team investigate a sprawling venue where apparitions, noises, and an unsavoury entity have been reported. Sara has an experience she won't forget. Dale gets a headache. And the entire team is mystified by a particular incident that keeps occurring.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Somebody walking there!
programme.desc: In this episode of the paranormal investigation series, the AYH team investigate a venue that has an intriguing history. Phil and Dale get a fright; Sara has a thought-provoking, emotional moment; and Jane becomes rather tongue-tied over it all.

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: Creeped out
programme.desc: The Are You Haunted team investigate a fascinating location, with a very varied history. Original AYH team member Tim rejoins the gang for the night, and everyone seems to be uneasy and on edge...but why?

programme.title: Are You Haunted..?: This doesn’t seem real!

programme.desc: It seemed like a match made in heaven when youth pastor Couty married a preacher's daughter, but when Couty meets his untimely end, a police investigation reveals a web of lies.

programme.desc: After dating for two months, Cynthia and Randy decide to elope, but tragedy strikes when one of them is found dead, leading police to investigate and discover a shocking truth.

programme.title: Happily Never After
programme.desc: Karen is murdered on her honeymoon cruise. Is she the victim of international espionage as her new husband claims, or is her assailant someone closer to home?

programme.title: Happily Never After
programme.desc: When Shannon and David meet, their instant attraction leads to a quick wedding. But after a tragic accident, the police must dig deep to expose a web of deceit.

programme.title: Happily Never After
programme.desc: Cupid's arrow hits the mark with Bruce and his gal Janice. But during a three-month anniversary hunting trip, a bullet shatters their union. But who fired it?

programme.title: Happily Never After
programme.desc: When single mom Debbie falls for salesman Bernard, they roll the dice and wed in Vegas. Their joy goes bust when the bride vanishes, and a fanatical secret obsession is uncovered.

programme.title: Happily Never After
programme.desc: Months after their wedding, Keith and Anita Yunk sleep peacefully in their bed when an attacker with a knife interrupts their slumber and hacks them to pieces.

programme.title: Happily Never After

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime: The Abbotsford Killer
programme.desc: Two girls are attacked by a man armed with a baseball bat. One of them manages to escape but the second one is found in a salmon river. The killer nags the police for months until he leaves behind a telling clue.

programme.title: Dark Waters of Crime
programme.desc: Following the gruesome discovery of a skeleton transported by water on an island, several detectives are challenged as they seek to unravel the unsolved mystery of the woman with no name.

programme.desc: The mother of two teenaged girls is found dead in her bathtub. The coroner declares it an accidental death, but a year later, new information prompt the police to reopen the case.

programme.desc: In April 1996, Winston Malcolm is rescued from icy waters of a lake after a canoe accident. He announced to hospital staff that his wife was with him. A dive team is called to the rescue, and the body of Jan Marshall is finally found a few days later.

programme.title: Naked Science: Atmosphere

programme.title: Naked Science: The Big Bang - The First Three Seconds
programme.desc: 13.7 billion years ago there was absolutely nothing. No space, no time, and no matter. Naked Science follows the first three seconds of creation when a speck of light appeared from nothing and rapidly expanded into an enormous Universe.
programme.sub-title: The Big Bang - The First Three Seconds

programme.desc: The Earth hurtles through space protected by an invisible force field, known as a magnetosphere.

programme.desc: Avoiding jetlag; a demonstration of the size of the solar system; square-wheeled motorbike.

programme.desc: Jem builds a laser; Yan recreates a demonstration to calculate the speed of light.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: The origins of weather; seasons allow life to form; Jem checks out wave power in Scotland.

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory

programme.title: Bang Goes The Theory
programme.desc: Homemade gules to test stickiness; puzzle with a scientific optical illusion; supersonic car.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: TORNADO
programme.desc: A tornado is a violently rotating column of air extending from the base of a thunderstorm down to the ground. Tornadoes are capable of completely destroying well-made structures, uprooting trees, and hurling objects through the air like deadly missiles.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: VOLCANOES
programme.desc: Volcanoes are Earth’s geologic architects. They’ve created more than 80 percent of our planet’s surface and their explosive force has crafted both mountains and craters.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MANMADE
programme.desc: When it comes to disasters, mother nature has mankind beat. However, as mankind expands, evolves and technology advances, it comes with dangerous challenges. When errors are made or unforeseen circumstances occur, the results can be devastating.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: EARTHQUAKES
programme.desc: Earthquakes happen when tectonic plates suddenly slip causing shock waves to shake the surface of the Earth. They occur on fault lines - the most active are the Ring of Fire in the Pacific Ocean and the San Andreas fault on America’s west coast.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: MEGAFLOODS
programme.desc: Water covers two thirds of the earth’s surface, and we are utterly dependent on it, but when this vital ingredient of life unleashes its fury on the planet it becomes the ultimate bringer of death.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: CONSTRUCTION
programme.desc: Bridges and tunnels have transformed the way we live, making the inaccessible easy to reach, and turning time consuming journeys into convenient commutes, but when things go wrong they can go very wrong indeed.

programme.title: Destruction Decoded: AIR, LAND AND SEA
programme.desc: Planes, trains and ships, our mass transit systems, have had a profound effect on mankind, making the world smaller and more connected.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Cosmic Rays
programme.desc: Cosmic rays can tell us much about the universe. They are not simply another form of light, but carriers of information about powerful and unseen processes at work thousands or even millions of light years away.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Fermi Paradox
programme.desc: The Fermi Paradox asks the ultimate question: Given a few reasonable assumptions about the chance of life arising elsewhere in the universe and the distance between the stars, why has an alien civilization not presented itself?

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landsat
programme.desc: Take a look at Landsat 8, the advanced planetary explorer with a 900 million dollar price tag! It is designed to peer across multiple wavelengths to reveal complex and ever-changing details that are undetectable by the human eye.

programme.title: Earthrise: The First Lunar Voyage
programme.desc: Interviews with Apollo 8 astronauts, their wives, mission control staff, and journalists give true insider's perspective to the space race of the late 1960's.

programme.title: How to Colonize the Stars
programme.desc: Leading physicists and engineers in the field of interstellar travel discuss some of the deep issues we face when considering the problem of sending spacecraft to other stars. Will mankind ever truly become a space fairing civilization?

programme.title: Hunting the Edge of Space: The Mystery of the Milky Way
programme.desc: The Mystery of the Milky Way chronicles the history of telescopes, from Galileo's refractor to Newton's reflector and beyond. This episode also looks at recent missions, including the Herschel Space Observatory's examination of the Milky Wa

programme.title: The New Detectives: Murder By Numbers
programme.desc: For serial killers, once is never enough. For investigators, the challenge is steep when the killers murder by numbers.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Stolen Identity
programme.desc: When a theft is committed, something valuable is stolen. But when a criminal needs a new identity, theft becomes a matter of life and death.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Predators And Parasites
programme.desc: For the forensic entomologist, the insects that nest in dead bodies are like tiny witnesses to a crime.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Fatal Impressions
programme.desc: 911 receives a desperate call in Fort Worth, Texas. A man's wife is shot. Forensic investigators search for clues in unlikely places, hoping the victim herself could provide information needed to determine how and why she died.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Drawing Conclusions
programme.desc: A teenager is abducted on a shopping trip. Two hikers disappear from the Appalachian Trail.

programme.title: The New Detectives
programme.desc: Solving crimes may begin with gut intuition, but advanced science provides investigators with irrefutable proof. When criminals go to great lengths to mask their crimes, Investigators must step up the challenge and remain forever undaunted.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Dead Men Do Talk
programme.desc: Profiles the work of world-renowned forensic experts and the procedures they use to solve murders and other mysteries in minutes or centuries after they happen.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Cold Cases
programme.desc: These cases took a decade or more to solve. There's no statute of limitations on murder. As a case turns cold, the clues become scarce, investigators must rely on science to close cold cases.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Blood Money
programme.desc: Some people murder for love. But these killers did it for the money. When greed is the motive, investigators must make every clue pay off.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Burning Evidence
programme.desc: Identifying Burned Remains: It's difficult to have a murder investigation without a body, and burning up the victim is a time-honored method of destroying physical evidence.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Written In Blood
programme.desc: In North Carolina, the home of a prominent couple becomes an unlikely scene of terrible bloodshed. Across the country, a California woman vanishes, worrying her family and the investigators trying to find her.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Witness To Terror
programme.desc: Black Boxes: Little evidence is left after an airplane takes a deadly plunge from the sky. Investigators' best hope for an answer comes from the flight data recorder known as a black box.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Double Helix
programme.desc: DNA Analysis: With the advent of DNA analysis, just a few microscopic cells found at a crime scene can be used to put a murder behind bars.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Cold Blooded
programme.desc: Time of death is an important consideration in a murder investigation, but when a killer freezes, burns, or grinds his victim, even the most skilled medical examiner would be at a loss about how to calculate it.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Medical Examiners's Casebook
programme.desc: Some killers choose to hide their victims. And investigators must then rely on forensic examiners to uncover proof of murder. These are just two extraordinary crimes that have made their way into the medical examiner's casebook.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Flames Of Justice
programme.desc: Arson fires are set for their own sake, or to cover a different crime, such as murder. Think fire consumes all vital clues? Think again. Arson investigators can glean important clues from scorched rubble and ignite the unquenchable flames of justice.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Presumed Dead
programme.desc: What does it take to prove murder if the victim cannot be found? Investigators must go to extreme lengths to catch the killer when the victim is presumed dead.

programme.title: The New Detectives: Collective Justice
programme.desc: Philadelphia's Vidocq Society, named after an 18th Century French detective, is one of the world's most unusual crime-solving organizations.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: The Patient Killer
